A walk down Walker RoadA short film: who I am, why I code, and the kind of AI I refuse to build.

The Founder

A creative who
learned to code.

Liam Sweeny has been a novelist and artist for years — and has spent eight years bringing the Capital Region's music scene to its own community, plus seventeen years in disaster response for the American Red Cross. He has a passion, and an enthusiasm, for putting technology to work for everyone's benefit.

And when he found his new love, there were suddenly two. Coding with AI was the other one.

Sweeny believes we can make AI beneficial. It doesn't have to take from artists without compensation or attribution. At Walker Road, we're developing innovations that will turn AI in the right direction — and until then, we build striking websites and custom apps that won't break the bank.

Who We Are & What We Do

Three things,
one principle.

Walker Road is built around a single idea — that emerging technology should be aimed at the global good before it's aimed at profit. Everything we do divides into three.

01

Values

Empowerment, first. Our work is governed by the Artist's Covenant — a binding set of commitments to attribution, consent, and provenance. Not a marketing line. Architecture.

The Artist's Covenant
02

Artists

Eight years of flyers, magazine covers, brand systems and event identities for the local music scene — bold, high-contrast, and unmistakably ours. The gallery speaks for itself.

Design portfolio
03

Products

We work with emergent tech and apply it to the global good first. When we explore new product design, we build the solution, release it free, and keep the template for our commercial work.

Flagship Free model
Good Governance

The Artist's Covenant

A consensual relationship between an AI and the people whose work feeds it. Commitments written into the license and enforced in the architecture itself — so they can't be quietly turned off later.

I.

Attribution

Every source is named. Creators are credited wherever their work travels.

II.

Consent

Nothing is used without a yes. Permission is the default, not the exception.

III.

Provenance

An unbroken, verifiable record of where every piece of work came from.

IV.

Source-Preservation

Originals are kept intact, never dissolved into an anonymous training soup.

V.

Tamper-Detection

Hash-sealed. If the covenant is altered, the system can tell — and so can you.

“It doesn't have to take from artists without compensation or attribution.” — the rule we build everything around.

The Art

Lines drawn
by hand.

Before the code, the pencil. A run of tribal line work and shaded studies — eyes, gears, crosses, mushroom clouds and DNA — drawn over the years. The same restless symbolism that still runs through everything Walker Road makes.

Selected Work

Eight years on
the wild side.

Brand systems, logos, book covers, websites, and a few hundred flyers for the people actually making the noise. A look at some of the most striking pieces.

Flagship Free

Build it. Solve it.
Give it away.

We work with emergent technology, and we're committed to seeing it applied first to the global good.

The Flagship Free Model

So when we explore new product design, we build solutions to real problems — and we release them for free, keeping the template for our own commercial work. Right now: websites and apps. More to come.
